children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Committee on Nonproliferation (in this section referred to as the “Committee”) is established as a committee of the National Security Council.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary of State.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary of Defense.

children.label: C
children.text: The Director of Central Intelligence.

children.label: D
children.text: The Attorney General.

children.label: E
children.text: The Secretary of Energy.

children.label: F
children.text: The Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.label: G
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: H
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce.

children.label: I
children.text: Such other members as the President may designate.
The National Coordinator for Nonproliferation Matters shall chair the Committee on Nonproliferation.

To review and coordinate Federal programs, policies, and directives relating to the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction and related materials and technologies, including matters relating to terrorism and international organized crime.

children.label: A
children.text: Integrated national policies for countering the threats posed by weapons of mass destruction.

children.label: B
children.text: Options for integrating Federal agency budgets for countering such threats.

children.label: C
children.text: Means to ensure that Federal, State, and local governments have adequate capabilities to manage crises involving nuclear, radiological, biological, or chemical weapons or related materials or technologies, and to manage the consequences of a use of such weapon or related materials or technologies, and that use of those capabilities is coordinated.

children.label: i
children.text: Preventing the smuggling of weapons of mass destruction and related materials and technologies.

children.label: ii
children.text: Promoting domestic and international law enforcement efforts against proliferation-related efforts.

children.label: iii
children.text: Countering the involvement of organized crime groups in proliferation-related activities.

children.label: iv
children.text: Safeguarding weapons of mass destruction materials and related technologies.

children.label: v
children.text: Improving coordination and cooperation among intelligence activities, law enforcement, and the Departments of Defense, State, Commerce, and Energy in support of nonproliferation and counterproliferation efforts.

children.label: vi
children.text: Improving export controls over materials and technologies that can contribute to the acquisition of weapons of mass destruction.

children.label: vii
children.text: Reducing proliferation of weapons of mass destruction and related materials and technologies.
